title: Contraption Lab
description: Build a Rube Goldberg chain-reaction machine — dominoes, seesaws, springs and fans that trigger each other, not a race track.
concept: A physics sandbox for chain-reaction machines: place ramps, dominoes, seesaws, springs, fans, bumpers, resting balls and a goal bell, then hit Run and watch one triggered event topple the next. Unlike a marble-race track, the point isn't speed — it's engineering a sequence where each part wakes the next (a falling domino row flings a resting ball, a seesaw catapults another) until the bell rings.
features: 8 placeable mechanism types (ramp, domino, seesaw, spring, fan, bumper, extra ball, goal bell) with a hand-rolled OBB/circle/segment collision engine
Build mode (drag-place, rotate, delete, move) and Run mode (fixed-timestep chain-reaction simulation with a settle-aware win/loss detector)
Domino-to-domino and domino-to-ball chain triggering via pendulum toppling physics, plus torque-driven seesaws and cooldown springs
Save/export/share: localStorage autosave + named saves, Blob/JSON export-import, btoa/atob shareable URL links, and Canvas toBlob PNG snapshots
Two built-in presets (Chain Starter, Domino Relay) plus a tap-to-poke interaction during Run for hands-on nudging
tech: Fixed-timestep physics loop with letterboxed logical-resolution viewport
Custom OBB-vs-circle and segment-vs-circle collision resolver
Pendulum-toppling domino physics with chain-reaction proximity triggers

interaction.mouse: Pick a part from the bottom palette and click the canvas to place it; drag while placing to position it. Select tool: click a part to select/move it, use the floating toolbar to rotate or delete.
interaction.click: Tap ▶ Run to launch the ball and watch the chain reaction; tap the canvas during Run to poke the ball with a small impulse.
interaction.touch: Full touch support for placing, dragging, rotating and erasing parts, plus tap-to-poke during simulation.
interaction.keyboard: 1-8 select a part tool, V = select tool, X = erase tool, R = run/reset, Delete/Backspace = delete selected part, Esc = deselect/close menu.
